description:
You are acting as a penetration tester who has just finished three Nessus-style scans that exported their findings as CSV files.  
All raw data has already been placed for you under  
  /home/user/scan_data/  

You must perform a short post-processing routine that involves running several commands in parallel, merging the raw CSVs, filtering high-risk findings, producing a JSON report, creating a service-level CSV summary, and leaving a reproducible processing log.

1. Input files (already present):
   • /home/user/scan_data/segment_a.csv  
   • /home/user/scan_data/segment_b.csv  
   • /home/user/scan_data/segment_c.csv  

   All three files have exactly the same header row:  
   ip,port,service,version,vuln_status,cvss_score,cve

2. Tasks to perform (order matters, but feel free to parallelise where it makes sense):

   a) Merge the three CSVs, preserving the header only once, into an intermediate file  
      /home/user/scan_work/merged_all.csv  

   b) From merged_all.csv create a filtered JSON file that ONLY contains rows where  
        vuln_status = “yes”  AND  cvss_score (numeric) ≥ 7.0.  
      Save it as  
        /home/user/scan_reports/high_risk_vulns.json  

      JSON format that the automated test will check:
        • Entire file is valid JSON.  
        • Top-level item is an array.  
        • Each element is an object holding exactly these keys  
            ip (string)  
            port (integer)  
            service (string)  
            version (string)  
            cvss_score (float)  
            cve (string)  
        • Objects must be sorted primarily by cvss_score in descending order,  
          and for ties by ip in ascending lexicographic order.  

   c) Create a CSV service summary, considering ALL rows whose vuln_status is “yes”
      (i.e. **not** only the high-risk subset).  
      Output path: /home/user/scan_reports/vuln_service_summary.csv  

      Required format (exactly, including column names and order):
        service,total_vulns,avg_cvss  
      One line per distinct service.  
      • total_vulns  – integer count of “yes” rows for that service.  
      • avg_cvss     – the arithmetic mean of cvss_score for that service,
                       rounded to two decimal places (e.g. 7.80).  
      • Lines must be sorted alphabetically by the service name.  
      • File must end with a single trailing newline.  

   d) Logging – every major step must append a line to  
        /home/user/scan_reports/process.log  
      Each line must follow this exact pattern (one space between timestamp and tag):
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS TAG  
      where TAG is one of  
        MERGE_COMPLETED  
        FILTER_COMPLETED  
        SUMMARY_COMPLETED  
      The three tags should appear in that order, one per line.  
      The timestamp must be the local system time when the step finished.

3. Concurrency requirement  
   The merge (step a) can be a single command, but steps b and c should execute in
   parallel from the same script/session (e.g. backgrounded or using GNU parallel/xargs -P).
   Ensure both finish successfully before the script exits, otherwise the
   process.log should not show the corresponding *_COMPLETED tag.

4. Directory expectations  
   Create the following directories if they do not already exist and make sure the
   agent has write access:  
     /home/user/scan_work  
     /home/user/scan_reports  

5. What the grader will check  
   • Existence and exact pathname of merged_all.csv, high_risk_vulns.json,
     vuln_service_summary.csv, and process.log.  
   • Exact JSON structure/order described above.  
   • Exact column names, order, rounding and sorting in the summary CSV.  
   • Presence and order of the three *_COMPLETED tags in process.log
     (timestamp values themselves are not tested, only their format).  
   • That steps b and c really ran concurrently (the two corresponding
     *_COMPLETED timestamps must differ by less than 5 seconds).  

Produce the files and logs exactly as specified. No additional output files are expected.

D) /home/user/scan_reports/process.log  
Three lines; examples (timestamps will differ):
2023-09-14 12:01:08 MERGE_COMPLETED
2023-09-14 12:01:10 FILTER_COMPLETED
2023-09-14 12:01:10 SUMMARY_COMPLETED

For concurrency verification the FILTER_COMPLETED and SUMMARY_COMPLETED timestamps must differ by <5 seconds.

Directory tree that must exist before the agent runs:
  /home/user/scan_data/   (contains the 3 CSVs above)
Agent has write permission to /home/user

The test harness will also ensure that /home/user/scan_work and /home/user/scan_reports either do not exist or are empty, so the agent must create/populate them.
